perturbation.original_text: (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Switzerland pertaining thereto and the Recipient agrees to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Canton of Geneva, Switzerland;
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of general administrative procedures, the recipient agrees to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Canton of Geneva, Switzerland. (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Switzerland pertaining thereto;
perturbation.explanation: The relocation of the jurisdictional clause makes it seem like it only applies to administrative procedures, not the enforcement of the entire agreement. This hides a key jurisdictional clause.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Swiss Federal Act on Private International Law (PILA)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 5 of the PILA requires clear and unambiguous choice of forum clauses, especially in international contracts. Separating the choice of law and choice of forum, and burying the latter in administrative procedures, creates ambiguity. Therefore, it becomes difficult to determine whether the parties truly agreed to the jurisdiction.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Trans-Lex.org This page uses so called "cookies" to improve its service (i.e. "tracking"). Learn more and opt out of tracking I agree Principles 1672 Bibliography 1035 Materials 86 Archive 66 History & Modern Evolution of Transnational Commercial Law The requested Document is not available. How to search - Contact - Team - Trustees - Disclaimer / Legal information / Privacy - Twitter - Facebook A project of CENTRAL , University of Cologne.
perturbation.justification: The relocation of the jurisdictional clause, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, undermines the clarity and enforceability of the agreement regarding jurisdiction. The Law Explanation further supports this by stating that Article 5 of the PILA requires clear and unambiguous choice of forum clauses. Separating the choice of law and choice of forum creates ambiguity, making it difficult to determine if the parties truly agreed to the jurisdiction. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is unavailable and Scraped Law Snippet 2 contains no relevant content, the other elements suggest a significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Definition of Confidential Information For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.changed_text:
Signed and agreed to this ___ day of ______________, 2015 __ ________________________________ ______________________________________ [typed name:_______________________] [type named and position: ________________] Recipient for the Association

Definition of Confidential Information For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of confidential information is moved to after the signature block, making it ambiguous whether the recipient actually agreed to the specific terms of what constitutes confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1 of the Swiss Code of Obligations requires mutual agreement between parties for a contract to be valid. By placing the definition of confidential information after the signature block, it is questionable whether the recipient had the opportunity to review and agree to the scope of confidentiality before signing the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Fedlex DE: Vielen Dank, dass Sie sie die Website des Bundesrechts aufgerufen haben; sie ist nur mit einem Javascript-fähigen Browser verfügbar. Um diese Website optimal nutzen zu können, empfehlen wir Ihnen, einen aktuellen Browser zu verwenden. FR: Merci d'avoir rejoint le site du droit fédéral; celui-ci n'est disponible qu'avec un navigateur supportant javascript. Afin de profiter d'une expérience maximale sur ce site, nous vous conseillons d'utiliser un navigateur récent. IT: Grazie per aver scelto il sito web del diritto federale; questo è disponibile soltanto con un browser che supporta JavaScript. Per navigare in modo ottimale in questo sito, vi consigliamo di utilizzare un browser più recente. RM: Grazia fitg che Vus visitais la pagina d’internet dal dretg federal; ella è disponibla mo cun in navigatur che sustegna javascript. Per pudair far diever da questa pagina d’internet en moda optimala As recumandain nus d’utilisar in navigatur actual. EN: Thank you for visiting the federal law website; it is only fully accessible if you are using a JavaScript-capable browser. In order to be able to use all the features of this site, we recommend that you use an up-to-date browser. DE: Vielen Dank, dass Sie sie die Website des Bundesrechts aufgerufen haben; sie ist nur mit einem Javascript-fähigen Browser verfügbar. Um diese Website optimal nutzen zu können, empfehlen wir Ihnen, einen aktuellen Browser zu verwenden. FR: Merci d'avoir rejoint le site du droit fédéral; celui-ci n'est disponible qu'avec un navigateur supportant javascript. Afin de profiter d'une expérience maximale sur ce site, nous vous conseillons d'utiliser un navigateur récent. IT: Grazie per aver scelto il sito web del diritto federale; questo è disponibile soltanto con un browser che supporta JavaScript. Per navigare in modo ottimale in questo sito, vi consigliamo di utilizzare un browser più recente. RM: Grazia fitg che Vus visitais la pagina d’internet dal dretg federal; ella è disponibla mo cun in navigatur che sustegna javascript. Per pudair far diever da questa pagina d’internet en moda optimala As recumandain nus d’utilisar in navigatur actual. EN: Thank you for visiting the federal law website; it is only fully accessible if you are using a JavaScript-capable browser. In order to be able to use all the features of this site, we recommend that you use an up-to-date browser.
perturbation.justification: The structural flaw introduces ambiguity regarding agreement to the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The Perturbation Explanation states the definition is moved after the signature block, raising questions about whether the recipient reviewed and agreed to the scope of confidentiality before signing. Although there are no scraped law snippets to confirm, the Perturbation explanation indicates that the change undermines the mutual agreement necessary for a contract to be valid by placing the definition after the signature. Therefore, it created a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (5) that the Recipient is aware of and has reviewed the ICoCA’s Conflict of Interest and Information Security policies and that Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person within the Association (i.e., the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members) if the disclosure of such information would violate the Association’s Conflict of Interest or Information Security policies;
perturbation.changed_text: (5) that the Recipient is aware of and has reviewed the ICoCA’s Conflict of Interest and Information Security policies;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase stating 'that Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person within the Association (i.e., the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members) if the disclosure of such information would violate the Association’s Conflict of Interest or Information Security policies,' the obligation of the agreement becomes ambiguous. The sentence implies there is awareness of policies, but does not restrict confidential information from being shared in violation of policy.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 4 of the Swiss Unfair Competition Act (UWG) prohibits the disclosure of business secrets. By removing the obligation not to disclose confidential information in violation of internal policies, the agreement becomes less effective in protecting against the unlawful disclosure of confidential business information, potentially undermining the UWG's purpose.

EN: Thank you for visiting the federal law website; it is only fully accessible if you are using a JavaScript-capable browser. In order to be able to use all the features of this site, we recommend that you use an up-to-date browser.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited the Recipient from disclosing Confidential Information to individuals within the Association if it violated conflict of interest or information security policies. The changed text removes this explicit prohibition, weakening the protection against unlawful disclosure of confidential business information. The Law Explanation states that Article 4 of the Swiss Unfair Competition Act (UWG) prohibits the disclosure of business secrets, indicating the importance of preventing such disclosures. By removing the restriction on disclosing confidential information in violation of internal policies, the modified agreement becomes less effective in preventing potential violations of UWG. Although the scraped snippets do not directly support this, the removal of the specific disclosure obligation directly impacts the enforcement and preventative measures related to the law.
